name: Habanerno Chicken Jerktoufee
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut 2 lb chicken breasts into small pieces.
2. Season the chicken with 3 TB Paul Prudhomme's Poultry Magic, 1 lb unsalted butter, and 1/2 c chopped green peppers, celery, yellow onion, and green onion.
3. Add 1 Habanero or Scotch Bonnet pepper (seeded and chopped), 1 jalapeno pepper (seeded and chopped), 2 TB Jamaican jerk sauce, 1 pn black pepper, 1 pn white pepper, 1 pn red pepper flakes, 1 pn salt, and 1 ts dry jerk seasoning to the chicken.
4. Heat 1/2 lb butter in a 4-quart saucepan until melted.
5. Add the chicken pieces and sauté until cooked through, about 7 minutes.
6. Remove the chicken and drain on paper towels.
7. Cook the vegetables with all seasonings over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 10 to 15 minutes until softened.
8. Make a roux by heating vegetable oil until smoking, then adding flour and stirring until caramel-colored.
9. Stir in the remaining butter gradually until incorporated.
10. Add cream, chicken stock, tomato sauce, cooked chicken, and tasso; cook until the liquid has reduced by half.
11. Serve over cornbread.
Yield: 4 servings
